title: helmet.hsts with maxAge: 0 — HSTS is disabled, leaving users open to HTTPS-downgrade (SSL-strip) attacks

params.passDetail: helmet.hsts sets a non-zero maxAge — browsers are told to use HTTPS for the site for that duration, preventing protocol downgrade.
params.failDetail: helmet.hsts is configured with maxAge: 0, which tells browsers to IMMEDIATELY EXPIRE the Strict-Transport-Security policy — HSTS is effectively off. A network attacker can strip TLS and serve the site over plain HTTP (SSL-strip), intercepting credentials and session cookies. Set maxAge to a real duration (e.g. 15552000 seconds = 180 days).
params.absentCallDetail: No helmet.hsts call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: helmet.hsts does not set maxAge; the helmet default (a non-zero max-age) applies.

provenance.note: maxAge: 0 reads as a harmless placeholder but actively tells browsers to forget the HTTPS-only policy. Pasted from a snippet that 'temporarily disables HSTS to test', it ships and reopens the SSL-strip window.
